undefined.name: Undefined
undefined.message: Undefined in diagnostic information usually means there is no problem here.
undefined.fixAdvice: You generally don't need to consider this diagnostic unless you notice other abnormal behavior.

unknownError.name: Unknown Error
unknownError.message: An unknown error occurred, possibly due to an internal error or an unhandled exception.
unknownError.fixAdvice: Please try restarting ASER Studio. If the problem persists, contact technical support and provide relevant logs.

ruleNotSelected.name: No Grammar Rule Selected
ruleNotSelected.message: Document %1 does not specify any grammar rule via the #useRule directive.
ruleNotSelected.fixAdvice: Try adding #useRule:%1 on the second line of the document to specify a grammar rule defined in %1.

noSuchRule.name: Grammar Rule Does Not Exist
noSuchRule.message: The grammar rule %2 specified by the #useRule directive in document %1 is invalid.
noSuchRule.fixAdvice: Check the parameter of the #useRule directive in document %1 to ensure it correctly specifies an existing grammar rule. Currently valid rules are: %2

noSuchController.name: Controller Does Not Exist
noSuchController.fixAdvice: Check that your code correctly references the controller prefix specified by rule %1, and ensure it is defined and spelled correctly.

missingRequiredParameter.name: Missing Required Parameter
missingRequiredParameter.message: Controller "%1 controller" is missing the required parameter %2.
missingRequiredParameter.fixAdvice: Remove the symbol indicating an optional parameter and re-enter the value starting from the line-start identifier.

parameterTypeMismatch.name: Parameter Type Mismatch
parameterTypeMismatch.message: Parameter %1 has a type mismatch. You may have provided type %2, but the expected type is %3.

parameterOutOfRange.name: Parameter Out of Range
parameterOutOfRange.message: The value of parameter %1 is outside the allowed range.
parameterOutOfRange.fixAdvice: Check the controller parameter and ensure its value is within the allowed range.

parameterFormatError.name: Parameter Format Error
parameterFormatError.fixAdvice: Check the controller parameter and ensure its format meets expectations.

unexceptedParameter.name: Unexpected Parameter
unexceptedParameter.fixAdvice: Check the definition of controller %1 to ensure it only accepts expected parameters and no extra parameters are passed.

unclosedSymbol.name: Unclosed Symbol
unclosedSymbol.message: Symbol %2 in document %1 is not properly closed.
unclosedSymbol.fixAdvice: Check symbol %2 in document %1 and ensure it is properly closed. (Add %3 to close it at an appropriate location.)

variableNotDefined.name: Variable Not Defined
variableNotDefined.fixAdvice: Check the spelling of %1, or ensure you have defined this variable earlier via the statement %1.

useTabInsteadSpace.name: Use Tab Instead of Space
useTabInsteadSpace.message: Spaces were used where tabs should be used.

useSpaceInsteadTab.name: Use Space Instead of Tab
useSpaceInsteadTab.message: Tabs were used where spaces should be used.
useSpaceInsteadTab.fixAdvice: Replace tabs with an appropriate number of spaces.

useEnglishPunctuation.name: Use English Punctuation
useEnglishPunctuation.message: Chinese punctuation was used where English punctuation is required.
useEnglishPunctuation.fixAdvice: Replace Chinese punctuation with the corresponding English punctuation.

useChinesePunctuation.name: Use Chinese Punctuation
useChinesePunctuation.message: English punctuation was used where Chinese punctuation is required.
useChinesePunctuation.fixAdvice: Replace English punctuation with the corresponding Chinese punctuation.

noSuchBlock.name: Block Does Not Exist
noSuchBlock.fixAdvice: Check the spelling of %1, or ensure you have defined this block earlier via the statement %1.

blockNameConflict.name: Block Name Conflict
blockNameConflict.message: Block %1 is already defined at line %2.
blockNameConflict.fixAdvice: Choose a different name for this block or the block already defined at line %1.

instrumentRedundant.name: Redundant Directive
instrumentRedundant.message: Directive %1 is redundant here because it duplicates a directive at line %1.

parameterRepeated.name: Parameter Repeated
parameterRepeated.message: Parameter %1 is defined again at column %2.
parameterRepeated.fixAdvice: Check the controller's parameter list and ensure each parameter appears only once.

unknownWarning.name: Unknown Warning
unknownWarning.message: An unknown warning occurred, possibly due to an internal error or an unhandled exception.
unknownWarning.fixAdvice: Please try restarting ASER Studio. If the problem persists, contact technical support and provide relevant logs.

nosuchResource.name: Resource Does Not Exist
nosuchResource.fixAdvice: Check the spelling of %1, or add a resource named %1 to your project.

useTabInsteadSpace_dialog.name: Use Tab Instead of Space in Dialog Controller
useTabInsteadSpace_dialog.message: In dialog controllers, use tabs (Tab key) to separate the speaker and dialog content instead of spaces. If you are sure the spaces are part of the speaker's name, ignore this warning.

Preprocessor.__description: Preprocessor directives, used to process the document during the preparation phase before playback.
Preprocessor.ruleName: Grammar rule name, used in the #useRule directive to define a grammar rule name
Preprocessor.blockName: Block name, used in the #block directive to define a block name, and in the #use directive to specify the block to be called
Preprocessor.versionNumber: Version name, used in the #version directive to specify the version of a controller or grammar rule
Preprocessor.param0: First parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param1: Second parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param2: Third parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param3: Fourth parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param4: Fifth parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param5: Sixth parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param6: Seventh parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param7: Eighth parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param8: Ninth parameter of the referenced block
Preprocessor.param9: Tenth parameter of the referenced block

Background.__description: Controls the display of the background.
Background.ImageName: Specifies the image name used for the background
Background.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the background, in seconds
Background.StartPosition: The starting coordinate position for the background image display
Background.EndPosition: The ending coordinate position for the background image display
Background.MoveTime: Time for the background image to move from start to end position, in seconds
Background.MoveCurve: Curve type for the background image movement
Background.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.
Background.StitchVertically: Whether to stitch multiple images vertically. true for vertical stitching, false for horizontal stitching.
Background.StitchColumnCount: Maximum number of stitches in the current stitching direction; exceeding this number automatically continues to the next column or row for grid-like stitching.

Music.AudioName: Specifies the audio name used for the music
Music.Volume: Specifies the volume of the music, ranging from 0 to 1
Music.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the music, in seconds
Music.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the music, default is 0 (i.e., track number). To play multiple music pieces simultaneously, use different slot indexes to distinguish them.
Music.Position: Spatial position of the music, -1 for left, 1 for right, adjustable within this range

SoundEffect.__description: Controls the playback of sound effects.
SoundEffect.AudioName: Specifies the audio name used for the sound effect
SoundEffect.Volume: Specifies the volume of the sound effect, ranging from 0 to 1
SoundEffect.Position: Spatial position of the sound effect, -1 for left, 1 for right, adjustable within this range
SoundEffect.Steps: Specifies the number of steps the sound effect lasts, i.e., how many controllers it continues for before terminating

Character.Character&Variant: Specifies the character name and variant; if no variant, use only the character name
Character.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the character, default is 0. To display multiple characters simultaneously, use different slot indexes to distinguish them.
Character.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.
Character.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the character, in seconds
Character.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the character, in seconds
Character.Effects: Specifies the effects for the character
Character.Alpha: Transparency of the character, ranging from 0 to 1
Character.RenderOrder: Render order of the character; characters with a higher value overlay those with a lower value.
Character.StartPosition: Starting coordinate position for the character display
Character.EndPosition: Ending coordinate position for the character display
Character.MoveTime: Time for the character to move from start to end position, in seconds
Character.MoveCurve: Curve type for the character movement

Choice.OutputVariable: Output variable name for the choice; the player's selection result will be stored in this variable

Delay.DelayTime: Duration of the delay, in seconds

VisualEffect.__description: Controls the display of visual effects.
VisualEffect.VisualEffectName: Specifies the effect name used for the visual effect
VisualEffect.Steps: Specifies the number of steps the visual effect lasts, i.e., how many controllers it continues for before terminating
VisualEffect.Position: Coordinate position of the visual effect
VisualEffect.RenderOrder: Render order of the visual effect; effects with a higher value overlay those with a lower value.

Interlude.Character&Variant: Specifies the character name and variant in the interlude; if no variant, use only the character name
Interlude.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the character in the interlude, default is 0. To display multiple characters simultaneously, use different slot indexes to distinguish them.
Interlude.Type: Type of interlude. 0 for grayscale vertical type, 1 for avatar type.
Interlude.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.
Interlude.Effects: Specifies the effects for the interlude
Interlude.RenderOrder: Render order of the interlude; interludes with a higher value overlay those with a lower value.

ColorMask.__description: Controls the display of color masks.
ColorMask.Color: Color of the mask, as a 4-dimensional vector
ColorMask.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the color mask, in seconds
ColorMask.StayTime: Specifies how long the color mask stays, in seconds
ColorMask.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the color mask, in seconds
ColorMask.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.

Shake.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the screen shake, in seconds
Shake.StayTime: Specifies how long the screen shake lasts, in seconds
Shake.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the screen shake, in seconds
Shake.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.

FreeText.__description: Controls the display of free text.
FreeText.IsAddition: Whether it is additional text. Additional text appends to existing text rather than replacing it.
FreeText.Immediate: Whether to display the text immediately instead of character by character
FreeText.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.
FreeText.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the text, in seconds
FreeText.StayTime: Specifies how long the text stays, in seconds
FreeText.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the text, in seconds
FreeText.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the text, default is 0. To display multiple text pieces simultaneously, use different slot indexes to distinguish them.
FreeText.AsSeceneObject: Whether to treat the text as a scene object, making it affected by other controllers in the scene, such as screen shakes and color masks.
FreeText.RenderOrder: Render order of the text; text with a higher value overlays that with a lower value.
FreeText.Interval: Time interval between the appearance of each character when displayed character by character, in seconds
FreeText.ExtraStayTime: Extra time the text stays after fully displayed, in seconds

Cut.Angle: Angle of the crop, in degrees
Cut.SlotIndex: Specifies the slot index for the crop, default is 0. To use multiple croppings simultaneously, use different slot indexes to distinguish them.
Cut.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.
Cut.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the crop, in seconds
Cut.StayTime: Specifies how long the crop lasts, in seconds
Cut.FadeOutTime: Specifies the fade-out time for the crop, in seconds
Cut.RenderOrder: Render order of the crop; crops with a higher value overlay those with a lower value.

Filter.Type&Intensity: Specifies the filter type and intensity; different types have different intensity ranges.
Filter.FadeInTime: Specifies the fade-in time for the filter, in seconds
Filter.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.

AnimatedText.__description: Controls the display of animated text.
AnimatedText.Type: Type of animated text; different types have different animation effects.
AnimatedText.IsBlocker: Whether it is blocking. Blocking controllers wait for all animations to finish before executing the next line.

Variable.Name: Name of the variable
Variable.Expression: Expression for the variable; the variable's value is evaluated as the result of this expression.

Judge.Name: Name of the variable where the judgment result will be stored
Judge.Expression: Judgment expression; if the result is greater than 0, it is stored in the variable.

Branch.Name: Name of the branch
Branch.Branches: The label names to jump to

Dialog.Speaker&Content: Speaker's name and dialog text content
Dialog.IsAdditional: Whether it is additional dialog. Additional dialog appends to existing dialog rather than replacing it.
Dialog.ModifyCharacters: Whether to synchronously control the display of existing character sprites to match changes in the speaker during the dialog, causing character sprites to automatically brighten/darken as the dialog proceeds.
Dialog.BasicStayTime: Base duration of the dialog, in seconds. The dialog will have additional duration based on text length added to this base time.
Dialog.Interval: Time interval between the appearance of each character when displayed character by character, in seconds
Dialog.ExtraStayTime: Extra time the dialog stays after fully displayed, in seconds

Comment.__description: Comment line, not executed by the playback engine.

Aliases.__description: Defines one or more aliases, allowing dialog controllers to automatically highlight corresponding sprites based on aliases.
Aliases.Name: Original name of the sprite material

window.title: ASE-Remake Story Project Initialization Assistant
window.where: The new project will be created in "%1".
window.exists: "%1" already exists or is not empty! Cannot create a new project here.

failed.title: Failed to Create Project
failed.text: Unable to create project. Please check whether the target folder is empty, or if you have permission to create new files in this folder.

name.title: Project Name
name.text: The name of the story project. If all characters are valid for file paths, it will also be the name of the project folder.

path.title: Project Path
path.text: A new project folder will be created at this path

version.title: Target ASE Version
version.text: By default, only the latest ASE version is used. If you need to use a specific older ASE version, associate the relevant version in the plugin settings.

window.title: AStoryX File Initialization Assistant
window.where: The new file will be created as "%1".
window.exists: "%1" already exists! Cannot create a new file.

failed.title: Failed to Create File
failed.text: Unable to create file. Please check whether the target folder is empty, or if you have permission to create new files in this folder.

astory.name: ASE Story File
astory.description: ASE story written with AStory, supports all ASE native features.
astory.title: AStoryX File Initialization

name.title: File Name
name.text: The name of the story file. If all characters are valid for file paths, it will also be the name of the file.

path.title: File Path
path.text: A new story file will be created at this path

version.title: Target ASE Version
version.text: By default, only the latest ASE version is used. If you need to use a specific older ASE version, associate the relevant version in the plugin settings.

exePath.title: Executable Path
exePath.text: Path to the ASE-Remake executable file.

version.title: ASE Version
version.text: The version of the ASE program you selected. If you are unsure of your ASE program's version, or if your ASE program is the latest version, select "Latest Version". Correct version settings allow ASER Studio to better compatibility with your ASE program and avoid potential issues.

windowSize.title: Window Size
windowSize.text: ASER Studio can specify window size options when launching ASE-Remake; these options are passed directly to the ASE-Remake program.

executableNotFound.title: Executable Not Found
executableNotFound.message: The configured path %1 is invalid and cannot launch the ASE-Remake application. Please check your configuration in File -> Plugins and Preferences -> ASER Studio Settings, and ensure you have correctly set the path to the ASE-Remake executable.

starting.title: ASE-Remake Beta Defect Notice
starting.message: Due to a known defect in the ASE-Remake beta, YSS can only launch ASE-Remake simply at this time, and cannot properly set the project directory or playback file. You will need to manually start playback. Starting debugging currently can only be used as a quick way to open the ASE-Remake program. Please check the changelog of the ASE-Remake beta and wait for this issue to be fixed.
